raw_text: pablo estrada fuertes address: calle nuestra seora del pilar 30,28911, leganés, madrid. spain. date of birth: 4" of december, 1992 contact: mobile phone +34 664064298 e-mail pabloestradafuertes @ gmail.com profile & objectives a hard-working, trustworthy and good student, ambitious enough to deal with challenging tasks and with a strong leadership capacity. an effective team member able to apply knowledge to unfamiliar problems, but also proactively, with good communication skills and excellent ability to quickly capture new topics. willing to acquire work responsibilities in aerospace or mechanical engineering positions to gain valuable experience. open to relocation, especially in foreign assignments. educational qualification e carlos iii university of madrid september 2015 present industrial engineering masters degree (until january, 2017). up to date average mark: 77 %. relevant coursework: - 1% semester: hydraulic machines (matlab for data analyzing), structural engineering (abaqus for design and calculation of 2d and 3d structures), electronics (lab practices: filter designs and microcontrollers). 2" semester: transport engineering (ansys for the design and calculation of a crane), thermal engines (ansys fluent for the design and calculation of a radial pump). 3" semester (current): advanced machines design (matlab for data processing), integrated manufacturing systems (abaqus as computer aided engineering software). e university of malaga october 2010 september 2015 industrial engineering bachelors degree (mechanical engineering major). average mark: 73,8 %. relevant coursework: fluid mechanics (ansys fluent for finite element method simulations for the flow over aerofoils), cad (autocad for building plans design and others), programming (c++), numerical methods with matlab, electronics (lab practices: analog integrated circuits), control design, strength of materials and elasticity, machines design. academic project: mathematical model to optimize the location of public bikes sharing stations. applied to the existing system in malaga. under prof. dr. josé juan nebro. mark: 98% with honours. tasks: definition of potential indicators on which the optimum location of a public bikes sharing station depends. generation of a delphi test, selecting a board of experts in the matter to mark the indicators. design of a mathematical model and implementation on excel. application to an existing public bikes sharing station system. e brno university of technology, exchange program february 2014 july 2014 relevant coursework: turbomachinery (lab practices: cavitation) and robots programming (lab practices: kuka robots). e university of guelph (canada), exchange program august 2013 january 2014 relevant coursework: digital systems design (vhdl for fpga implementation), german. professional experience e deloitte consulting october 2015 december 2015 engineering intern 4th edition of the course "deloitte technology information challenge" (dtic). theoretical lessons regarding the important role technology plays on information systems nowadays and its application to business. problem-solving team structure in order to deal with real projects previously carried out by the company. technical skills programming: c++, vhdl, python (beginner). tools known: microsoft word & excel, latex, autocad, ansys, abaqus, catia (catia v5: mechanical design, currently in ingnova academy online course), matlab. languages spanish mother tongue english advanced level. c1 cambridge certificate (october 2014) german basic level. four-month a2 course (december 2013) extracurricular activities + awards teacher of english in enforex, alboran summer camp 2016. shop assistant in zara la cafiada, marbella (summers of 2014 and 2015). private teacher of physics, maths and technical drawing for three years (2012-2015). football player for 16 years. champion of the spanish cup with the andalusian team in 2006. edx certificate for the course introduction to management information systems (mis): a survival guide. personal interests and hobbies - football, playing the guitar and reading. - aerospace world. - travelling abroad and being always open to learning new things. page 2 of 2
